name: SQL Injection via SqlCommand with string concatenation
description: User input flows into a SqlCommand constructor or ExecuteReader call as part of a concatenated or interpolated SQL string, without the use of parameterized queries. An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL, reading or modifying database contents.
remediation: Always use parameterized queries with SqlParameter or cmd.Parameters.AddWithValue. Never concatenate or interpolate user input directly into SQL strings. Treat $"SELECT ... {value}" as unsafe unless `value` is an untainted constant.
